key_insight: The paper introduces the Patnaik-Pearson intrinsic dimension, a new way to quantify the geometry of neural-network data manifolds and internal representations, with special attention to transformer embeddings and weight spectra.
engineering_relevance: It gives practitioners a principled metric for tracking how representation complexity changes across layers, which can help analyze model compression, scaling, and feature collapse in transformers.
method: Defines a new intrinsic-dimension estimator inspired by TwoNN and earlier HTSR/SETOL theory.
Proves mathematical properties of the estimator and relates it to power-law empirical spectral densities of weight matrices.
Applies the metric to token embeddings and layerwise representations in BERT-base and DeepSeek-R1-Distill-Qwen-1.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports both theoretical proofs and numerical experiments on transformer models, including initial token embeddings and their evolution through layers.
watch_item: The method’s interpretation may depend on assumptions such as power-law spectral behavior and careful numerical estimation, so results should be checked for sensitivity to model, layer, and sampling choices.
paper_title: Patnaik-Pearson intrinsic dimension for internal representations of neural networks
